days.overnight: Oak Bay anchorage or Victoria Harbour
highlights: Clear Canadian customs via CBSA — call 1-888-226-7277 on arrival or pre-clear with ArriveCAN app
Top off fuel at Westbay Marina or Coast Victoria Harbourside if desired
Walk the Inner Harbour: Empress Hotel, Parliament Buildings lit at dusk
Dinner in downtown Victoria — Fishhook or The Tapa Bar are standouts
Anchor in Oak Bay for a quiet night away from Inner Harbour ferry traffic
days.note: Depart early — US/Canada customs by water can involve a wait. Passports for everyone aboard.

days.overnight: False Creek Fisherman's Wharf or Granville Island Marina
highlights: Short 28 NM run from Victoria to Ganges Harbour, Salt Spring Island
Ganges Saturday Market (April–October): 2–3 hrs ashore, local produce, artisan food and crafts
Quick dinghy or paddleboard of Ganges Harbour before the afternoon push north
55 NM to False Creek — arrive Vancouver mid/late afternoon
Fuel at False Creek Fuel Dock; major provisioning at Granville Island Public Market
days.note: Depart Salt Spring by 1:30–2pm to arrive Vancouver before evening. If you time it for a Saturday, the Ganges market is not to be missed.

highlights: Full day: Science World, Stanley Park Aquarium, English Bay beach for the kids
Top up provisions for 3–4 days — next reliable stop is Refuge Cove or Powell River
West Marine and chandleries nearby for any gear
Check Environment Canada weather for the Howe Sound / Jervis Inlet run north
Dinner: Hawksworth, Ancora Waterfront Dining, or water taxi to Lonsdale Quay
Confirm Malibu Rapids slack window for Day 4 arrival
days.note: Use today to nail down Malibu Rapids timing. Plan your Day 4 departure from Vancouver to arrive exactly at the slack window — not the other way around.

days.overnight: Princess Louisa Marine Provincial Park (anchor / stern-tie)
days.tideNote: Malibu Rapids: passable ONLY within ±30 min of slack water. Plan your Vancouver departure to arrive at the exact slack window.
highlights: Depart Vancouver early — Howe Sound is glassy in the morning before sea breeze fills in
North up the Strait of Georgia via Malaspina Strait into Jervis Inlet
Jervis Inlet is one of the great fjords of BC — 800m deep, walls rising to snow, waterfalls everywhere
Time your arrival at Malibu Rapids to the exact slack using XTide, Navionics, or the CHS app
Anchor inside Princess Louisa Marine Provincial Park — stern-tie rings at the park dock near Chatterbox Falls
days.note: Malibu Rapids is the most critical passage of the trip. No safe transit exists except within ±30 min of slack. Build the entire day around this window.

highlights: Hike to Chatterbox Falls — short trail, spectacular in July with snowmelt still running
Kayak or paddleboard the full 8km inlet — crystal-clear water, walls rising straight to snow
Fish for cutthroat trout near the river head (BC freshwater regulations apply)
Wildlife: black bears on the shoreline, bald eagles overhead, seals common
Sundowners in the cockpit watching waterfalls — this is the highlight of the whole trip
days.note: One of the most spectacular anchorages in the world. Fully protected from swell. Don't rush it.

days.overnight: Prideaux Haven, Desolation Sound Marine Park
days.tideNote: Exit Malibu Rapids at slack — same timing discipline as entry. No exceptions.
highlights: Exit Malibu at slack tide, then north through Agamemnon Channel
Optional Powell River stopover: bakery, fuel top-off, provisions if needed
Desolation Sound has the warmest sea water north of Mexico — July temps hit 24°C (75°F)
Prideaux Haven: a maze of islets and coves with stern-tie rings throughout the marine park
Kayak the labyrinth in the evening light — among BC's finest anchorages
days.note: Prideaux fills fast in July — arrive by early afternoon. Tenedos Bay (adjacent) is an excellent overflow anchorage.

highlights: Swim directly from the boat — 24°C water in July is genuinely warm, not just tolerable
Kayak the inner maze of Prideaux Haven at high tide — accessible islets and hidden coves
Fish for coho or bottom fish from the dinghy — Desolation Sound holds good stocks
Day trip to Laura Cove or Grace Harbour by dinghy
Best stargazing of the trip — minimal light pollution, long July twilight gives way to a spectacular sky
days.note: This layover is well earned. Desolation Sound gets its name from a gloomy 1792 survey — ignore that. In July it's the warmest, most beautiful anchorage on the BC coast.

days.leg: Desolation Sound → Campbell River (fuel) → Port Neville
days.overnight: Port Neville anchorage or Thurston Bay Marine Park
days.tideNote: Seymour Narrows is 5 NM north of Campbell River — 16-knot peak currents. Coordinate your fuel stop timing so you hit the Narrows at slack. Plan the whole day around this window.
highlights: Depart Desolation early (7am) — arrive Campbell River ~10:30am at 15 kts for fuel and a quick stretch
Fuel at Discovery Harbour Marina and grab provisions — no overnight here
Time departure from Campbell River to hit Seymour Narrows at slack (5 NM north)
Once through the Narrows, the worst is behind you — beautiful Johnstone Strait opens up
Port Neville: remote, peaceful anchorage at the mouth of a long inlet — excellent crabbing and prawning
days.note: This is a coordinated day: depart Desolation early, fuel Campbell River at ~10:30am, Seymour Narrows at slack midday, anchor Port Neville by mid-afternoon. At 15 kts this is ~7.5 hrs total but the Narrows wait may be built in. Brief lunch at Campbell River fuel dock.

days.overnight: Telegraph Cove Marina or nearby anchorage
highlights: Easy run through Johnstone Strait — one of the world's premier orca corridors
July is peak resident orca season — scan constantly, pods are frequently sighted
Humpbacks, minkes, Steller sea lions, and Dall's porpoises common throughout Johnstone Strait
Telegraph Cove: charming historic boardwalk fishing village, great for a walk and lunch ashore
Stubbs Island Whale Watching runs expert-guided orca tours from the dock if you want a guaranteed experience
days.note: Relaxed day after yesterday's long push. Take your time through Johnstone Strait — the orca viewing here is as good as it gets on the BC coast.

days.overnight: Lagoon Cove Marina, East Cracroft Island
highlights: Short run — arrive Lagoon Cove by late morning, get settled before the afternoon action
Fuel at Lagoon Cove Marina — one of the most beloved stops in the Pacific Northwest
Explore the Broughton Archipelago by kayak or dinghy from the marina — countless channels to explore
Orca sightings are frequent in this area in July — ask Kelly & Dan for current reports
Book Knight Inlet grizzly tour for tomorrow if you haven't already
days.note: Arriving early gives you the afternoon to explore and get oriented. Tonight: Kelly & Dan's famous prawn potluck — they supply the prawns, you bring an appetizer. Unmissable.

highlights: Knight Inlet / Glendale Cove grizzly bear watching — July is peak season, multiple bears near shore feeding on vegetation and fish
Orca hotspot: Johnstone Strait and the Broughton channels are among the best orca corridors in the world
Kayak the archipelago — some of the most intricate island maze paddling in BC
Fish for lingcod, rockfish, and salmon in the channels around East Cracroft Island
Explore Echo Bay — Pierre's floating post office, a slice of remote BC character
days.note: Book Knight Inlet Lodge or Tide Rip Grizzly Tours in advance — this is a genuine bucket-list experience. Seeing a grizzly from the water in its natural habitat with the kids is unforgettable.

days.overnight: Blind Channel Resort, West Thurlow Island
highlights: Blind Channel Resort: one of BC's most charming remote marina destinations
Their cinnamon rolls are legendary — get in line early at the bakery or they sell out
Excellent restaurant for dinner: fresh local seafood, full bar, family-friendly atmosphere
Hiking trails directly from the dock: old-growth forest, panoramic viewpoints
Well-stocked general store — last good provisioning point before the south run
days.note: Call ahead on VHF 66A to reserve a slip — Blind Channel fills up in summer. Easy run today; enjoy the slow pace.

highlights: Squirrel Cove: a classic, well-sheltered anchorage with a beautiful enclosed tidal lagoon
Walk to the Squirrel Cove General Store — ice cream, basic provisions, friendly island atmosphere
Warm swimming — you're back in Desolation Sound warmth territory here
Kayak the inner lagoon at high tide — a gorgeous hidden basin accessible through a narrow passage
Fish for coho salmon returning through this corridor in July
days.note: Gentle re-entry to the southern waters. The lagoon inside Squirrel Cove is one of those hidden BC gems — explore it by kayak or dinghy at high tide.

days.overnight: Montague Harbour Marine Provincial Park, Galiano Island
highlights: South through the Discovery Islands and upper Gulf Islands
Transit Active Pass carefully — BC Ferries run constantly and have right of way
Montague Harbour: one of BC's most beautiful marine park anchorages, famous for its shell beach
Walk the Montague Harbour trails to the shell middens and headland views
Hummingbird Pub on Galiano Island for a relaxed family dinner ashore
days.note: Active Pass: narrow channel between Galiano and Mayne Islands with heavy BC Ferries traffic. Reduce speed, hug the correct side, and give ferries wide berth.

days.overnight: Montague Harbour or Dionisio Point, Galiano Island
highlights: Hike Dionisio Point Provincial Park — dramatic sandstone bluffs, arbutus forest, sweeping views of the Gulf Islands
Kayak or paddleboard the calm harbour — excellent for all ages
Fishing: chinook and coho salmon returning through this corridor in July
Dinghy to the Montague Harbour village: small café, general store, easy island pace
Afternoon swim off the shell beach — warm, shallow, ideal for kids
days.note: First full Gulf Islands decompression day. No agenda required.

days.overnight: Prevost Island, Wallace Island, or Ganges Harbour (Salt Spring)
highlights: Day cruise by dinghy or kayak to Wallace Island Marine Park — one of BC's most beautiful uninhabited islands
Or motor back to Salt Spring (Ganges) for town time — shops, restaurants, evening on the dock
Prevost Island anchorage: secluded, no crowds, exceptional dinghy exploration of the coves
Beachcomb, fish from the swim step, paddle at sunset — full unstructured family day
Move anchorages mid-day if the mood strikes
days.note: Second Gulf Islands layover. No agenda — use it however the family wants. Salt Spring Ganges is excellent if anyone wants shops or a proper meal ashore.

highlights: Depart Gulf Islands in the morning — easy run south to Victoria via Haro Strait
Fuel top-off at Victoria Harbour before the crossing (optional but recommended)
Victoria Fisherman's Wharf: final Canadian lunch — outdoor fish & chips, harbour seals at the dock
Clear US Customs via CBP ROAM app before entering US waters — passports ready for all aboard
Haro Strait crossing: aim to cross before 2pm to stay ahead of afternoon northwest wind
Optional: brief stop at Roche Harbor (San Juan Islands) to break the final leg
days.note: Slightly over the usual 100 NM preference, but Victoria breaks it naturally into two segments. Register everyone on the CBP ROAM app before leaving Victoria. Shilshole or Lake Union via the locks for Seattle landfall.

9.name: Telegraph Cove Historic Boardwalk
9.notes: Stroll the preserved 1920s cannery boardwalk with original buildings, museums, and gift shops. A unique step back into BC's fishing history.

9.name: Whale Interpretive Centre
9.notes: Learn about orcas, humpbacks, and other marine mammals in these waters. Exhibits cover local whaling history and modern conservation efforts.

9.name: Telegraph Cove Marina Restaurant/Pub
9.notes: Waterfront dining with fresh seafood and views of the boardwalk. Popular spot for locals and cruisers.

9.name: Whale Watching Tour (morning departure)
9.notes: July is peak season for orca sightings in Broughton Strait. Multiple operators offer 4–6 hour guided excursions from the cove.

9.name: Telegraph Cove Marina
9.notes: Full-service facility with fuel, water, and provisioning. Great base for exploring the North Island's marine habitat.

10.name: Lagoon Cove Marina
10.notes: Protected anchorage on East Cracroft Island with good holding and stunning wilderness scenery. Excellent base for exploring northern Vancouver Island's remote inlets.

10.name: Broughton Strait Wildlife Viewing
10.notes: Prime orca and humpback whale watching area during summer months. The nutrient-rich waters attract marine mammals and seabirds throughout July.

10.name: Kwakwaka'wakw Cultural Sites
10.notes: Explore Indigenous heritage and historic village sites in the area. Telegraph Cove nearby has cultural museums and displays of First Nations history.

10.name: Remote Hiking & Kayaking Trails
10.notes: Access pristine temperate rainforest and coastal paddling from the cove. Day trips to nearby inlets offer solitude and untouched natural beauty.

9: Peak whale-watching season—orcas and humpbacks actively feed in nearby Broughton Strait
Authentic 1920s cannery village atmosphere; one of BC's best-preserved maritime heritage sites
Remote Inside Passage location offering pristine wilderness and solitude
10: Exceptional marine wildlife viewing—some of BC's best orca encounter rates in July
Pristine, sheltered anchorage in a true wilderness setting with minimal crowds
Gateway to explore remote fjords and First Nations cultural heritage
Ideal for kayaking, hiking, and nature photography in old-growth forests

notes.4:
Malibu Rapids is the most critical passage of the trip. No safe transit exists except within ±30 min of slack. Build the entire day around this window.
